mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2008-12-15 indicates emergency monetary policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration. When payroll losses exceed 500K persons in a single month, equity investors anticipate recession deepening and reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135,489K as of 2008-12-15 reflects ongoing job losses in financial crisis
regime_hint: recession with accelerating labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 500K, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously over a 3-month period, it indicates demand destruction across housing and consumption. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 5% over 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 625K and RSAFS at 343,242M reflect demand collapse in late 2008
regime_hint: demand-driven recession with synchronized contraction across housing and retail
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 5% over 3 months, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and falling corporate profitability. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.12 as of 2008-12-15 reflects manufacturing contraction
regime_hint: supply-side recession with declining industrial capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO is below its 12-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 7% signals labor market stress that reduces consumer confidence and spending power. When UNRATE crosses above 7%, equity investors anticipate lower future consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.2% as of 2008-12-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market stress regime with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 7%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month signals deflationary pressure. When CPI falls for 2+ consecutive months, it indicates demand collapse and rising real debt burdens. Equity investors anticipate lower nominal earnings and higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.06 as of 2008-11-15 reflects deflationary pressures in late 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following 2+ months of CPI decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure aggregate demand momentum. When GDPC1 growth turns negative (quarter-over-quarter annualized) and RSAFS declines simultaneously, it signals recession entry. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 0% annualized and RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,712.4B and RSAFS at 343,242M reflect recession conditions in Q4 2008
regime_hint: recession entry with synchronized demand collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both GDPC1 < 0% and RSAFS declines > 2%, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously over 3 months, it signals broad-based supply-side contraction and falling capacity. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings misses.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 2% and PAYEMS declines > 300K persons over 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:Both INDPRO and PAYEMS show contraction in late 2008 financial crisis
regime_hint: supply-side recession with synchronized production and employment collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO declines > 2% and PAYEMS declines > 300K, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
